title: hieracium pulchellum subsp. pleioscapum
intro: Hieracium pulchellum subsp. pleioscapum, also known as Dwarf Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its small yellow daisy-like flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Hieracium pulchellum subsp. pleioscapum include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its usage should be carefully monitored due to potential interactions with other medications.
